title: Shadows over Phandalin
subtitle: Family-friendly starter, prepared sessions and a living town
description: Frontier sample campaign designed to teach session prep, clear clues, memorable NPCs, town consequences and the player portal.
summary: Phandalin is a frontier town built over old ruins, unsafe roads and mining promises. Gundren’s disappearance pushes the party toward goblins, bandits, frightened neighbors and a threat still operating from afar.
recommendedFor: DMs who want to try DMCC with an easy-to-run table campaign, ideal for families or new groups: prepared scenes, clear goals, public material for the player portal and enough secrets to practice revelations without overwhelming the table.
pitch: A starter campaign for learning DMCC through a comfortable structure: central town, clear quests, memorable NPCs, visible clues and consequences that make players feel Phandalin changing with their decisions.
learningGoals: Prepare classic adventure sessions with goals, clues and a short checklist.
Use the town as a living hub where each NPC can react to the party’s actions.
Separate public clues, DM secrets and consequences that change Phandalin’s tone.
Publish safe player-facing information without revealing the whole mystery.
includedMaterial: Five prepared sessions from the opening ambush to the lost mine.
Town NPCs, factions, locations, clues, rumors and table handouts.
Consequence arcs for freeing Phandalin or letting fear grow.
A threat canvas for reading Redbrands, Gundren and the Black Spider at a glance.

quickStart.title: How to start Shadows over Phandalin
steps: Open the fronts canvas and find three axes: missing Gundren, Redbrands in town and the mine as a future promise.
Run session 1 as a simple adventure: ambush, clear clue and a choice to follow tracks or protect supplies.
In session 2, use town NPCs so every player has someone to talk to.
When the party acts against the Redbrands, record how Phandalin’s mood changes as a consequence.

ent_tpl_phandalin_note.title: How to use Phandalin in DMCC
ent_tpl_phandalin_note.summary: This example teaches DMCC’s most direct flow: prepare a session, run clear scenes, record clues and let the town react to the party.
ent_tpl_phandalin_note.content: This template is designed for family tables: clear goals, recognizable NPCs and simple decisions with visible consequences.

ent_tpl_phandalin_town.title: Phandalin
ent_tpl_phandalin_town.subtitle: Frontier town
ent_tpl_phandalin_town.summary: A small settlement trying to prosper while living under external threats and internal intimidation.

ent_tpl_phandalin_trail.title: Triboar Trail
ent_tpl_phandalin_trail.summary: The road where the party finds the first signs that Gundren has been attacked.

ent_tpl_phandalin_hideout.title: Cragmaw Hideout
ent_tpl_phandalin_hideout.summary: A goblin cave that teaches the group Gundren’s disappearance was not random.

ent_tpl_phandalin_tresendar.title: Tresendar Manor
ent_tpl_phandalin_tresendar.summary: Ruins hiding the Redbrand base and several moral choices for the group.

ent_tpl_phandalin_wave_echo.title: Wave Echo Cave
ent_tpl_phandalin_wave_echo.summary: Final destination of Gundren’s search and the heart of the conflict over the Forge of Spells.

ent_tpl_phandalin_gundren.title: Gundren Rockseeker
ent_tpl_phandalin_gundren.subtitle: Dwarven prospector
ent_tpl_phandalin_gundren.summary: He hired the party to escort supplies. His map is the piece everyone wants.

ent_tpl_phandalin_sildar.title: Sildar Hallwinter
ent_tpl_phandalin_sildar.subtitle: Veteran and potential ally
ent_tpl_phandalin_sildar.summary: He can explain the context and give the group a reason to protect Phandalin.

ent_tpl_phandalin_redbrands.title: Redbrands
ent_tpl_phandalin_redbrands.subtitle: Red-cloaked thugs
ent_tpl_phandalin_redbrands.summary: They control the town’s fear through low-intensity violence and public threats.

ent_tpl_phandalin_iarno.title: Iarno Albrek / Glasstaff
ent_tpl_phandalin_iarno.subtitle: Missing wizard with a double identity
ent_tpl_phandalin_iarno.summary: Sildar’s clue points to a lost ally, but the truth connects him to the Redbrands.

ent_tpl_phandalin_nezznar.title: Nezznar, the Black Spider
ent_tpl_phandalin_nezznar.summary: He wants to reach Wave Echo Cave first and control the Forge.

ent_tpl_phandalin_quest_gundren.title: Find Gundren
ent_tpl_phandalin_quest_gundren.summary: The main thread: follow the trail from the ambush to Cragmaw and eventually the mine.

metadata.objective: rescue Gundren and recover the map

ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_dead_horses.title: Dead horses on the road
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_dead_horses.summary: The first physical clue: the attack was prepared and Gundren was ahead of the party.

ent_tpl_phandalin_secret_iarno.title: Iarno is Glasstaff
ent_tpl_phandalin_secret_iarno.summary: Sildar’s missing ally leads or manipulates the Redbrands from Tresendar.

ent_tpl_phandalin_consequence_town.title: Phandalin finds its voice again
ent_tpl_phandalin_consequence_town.summary: If the Redbrands fall, the town starts acting, speaking and asking for help in visible ways.

ent_tpl_phandalin_inn.title: Stonehill Inn
ent_tpl_phandalin_inn.subtitle: Safe place for conversations
ent_tpl_phandalin_inn.summary: Social hub where the party can hear rumors, rest and measure the town’s fear.
ent_tpl_phandalin_inn.content: Use it to introduce NPCs without forcing scenes: someone is always listening, exaggerating or asking for help.

ent_tpl_phandalin_shrine.title: Shrine of Luck
ent_tpl_phandalin_shrine.subtitle: Small refuge with useful information
ent_tpl_phandalin_shrine.summary: A calm place where a kind conversation can provide clues about disappearances and night movements.
ent_tpl_phandalin_shrine.content: Good place for players seeking help without going through violence.

ent_tpl_phandalin_toblen.title: Toblen Stonehill
ent_tpl_phandalin_toblen.subtitle: Innkeeper who knows more than he wants to admit
ent_tpl_phandalin_toblen.summary: He hears everyone’s rumors, but fears the Redbrands will punish his family.
ent_tpl_phandalin_toblen.content: Toblen helps if the group shows they are not here just to provoke a fight.

ent_tpl_phandalin_linene.title: Linene Graywind
ent_tpl_phandalin_linene.subtitle: Pragmatic merchant
ent_tpl_phandalin_linene.summary: She wants safe roads and does not trust improvised heroes until she sees results.
ent_tpl_phandalin_linene.content: She can offer gear, information about lost supplies or a dry warning about the Redbrands.

ent_tpl_phandalin_halia.title: Halia Thornton
ent_tpl_phandalin_halia.subtitle: Ambitious, useful and dangerous
ent_tpl_phandalin_halia.summary: She may help against the Redbrands if that increases her influence over Phandalin.
ent_tpl_phandalin_halia.content: Halia gives practical information, but always tries to keep a political advantage.

ent_tpl_phandalin_qelline.title: Qelline Alderleaf
ent_tpl_phandalin_qelline.subtitle: Kind neighbor with discreet contacts
ent_tpl_phandalin_qelline.summary: She knows less-watched roads and can point the group toward outside help.
ent_tpl_phandalin_qelline.content: Ideal for family tables: offers warmth, food and a nonviolent way to gain direction.

ent_tpl_phandalin_quest_break_fear.title: Break the Redbrands’ fear
ent_tpl_phandalin_quest_break_fear.subtitle: Urban arc of Phandalin
ent_tpl_phandalin_quest_break_fear.summary: The group must move from hearing complaints to giving the town a concrete reason to act.
ent_tpl_phandalin_quest_break_fear.content: It is not solved only by defeating enemies: the town changes when it sees protection, proof or leadership.

ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_blackseal.title: Note with a black seal
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_blackseal.subtitle: Clue toward a distant hand
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_blackseal.summary: A short note orders Gundren kept alive until the full map is found.
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_blackseal.content: It does not reveal everything about Nezznar, but introduces that the Redbrands are not the end of the chain.

metadata.content: The order comes from someone signing with a black spider, not from Glasstaff.

ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_redclay.title: Boots with red clay
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_redclay.subtitle: Physical clue in town
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_redclay.summary: Several bandit boots carry clay from the old path toward Tresendar.
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_redclay.content: Lets the group find the base without relying on interrogations.

metadata.content: The clay matches the abandoned path below Tresendar Manor.

ent_tpl_phandalin_rumor_well.title: The old well sings at night
ent_tpl_phandalin_rumor_well.subtitle: Rumor to color the town
ent_tpl_phandalin_rumor_well.summary: Children and miners say the well returns echoes that belong to no one in town.
ent_tpl_phandalin_rumor_well.content: It can be local color or a soft clue toward old ruins under Phandalin.

ent_tpl_phandalin_handout_map.title: Gundren’s torn map
ent_tpl_phandalin_handout_map.subtitle: Player handout
ent_tpl_phandalin_handout_map.summary: Map fragment with an incomplete mark toward Wave Echo Cave.
ent_tpl_phandalin_handout_map.content: Give only one part at first. The second part can appear in Tresendar or with Gundren.

ent_tpl_phandalin_encounter_street.title: Fight on the main street
ent_tpl_phandalin_encounter_street.subtitle: Flexible encounter against thugs
ent_tpl_phandalin_encounter_street.summary: The Redbrands test whether the group can be intimidated in front of frightened neighbors.
ent_tpl_phandalin_encounter_street.content: Can be resolved through combat, public humiliation, hard negotiation or a social trap.

ent_tpl_phandalin_consequence_people_ris.title: The town starts organizing
ent_tpl_phandalin_consequence_people_ris.subtitle: Positive consequence
ent_tpl_phandalin_consequence_people_ris.summary: If the group protects specific neighbors, Phandalin stops waiting for saviors and starts acting.
ent_tpl_phandalin_consequence_people_ris.content: Trigger this consequence when players help a named person, not just when they win a fight.

ent_tpl_phandalin_secret_nezznar_funds.title: The Black Spider funds chaos from afar
ent_tpl_phandalin_secret_nezznar_funds.subtitle: Campaign secret
ent_tpl_phandalin_secret_nezznar_funds.summary: Nezznar does not need to control Phandalin directly; he only needs nobody looking toward the mine.
ent_tpl_phandalin_secret_nezznar_funds.content: Lets the Redbrands be a local problem connected to a larger threat without turning everything into exposition.

metadata.truth: Nezznar uses intermediaries to keep the town distracted while he searches for Wave Echo Cave.

rel_tpl_phandalin_trail_hideout.description: The tracks lead from the road to the goblin hideout.

rel_tpl_phandalin_gundren_quest.description: Finding Gundren keeps the campaign moving.

rel_tpl_phandalin_hideout_gundren.description: The hideout reveals that Gundren was moved.

rel_tpl_phandalin_redbrands_town.description: The town avoids acting because it fears retaliation.

rel_tpl_phandalin_redbrands_tresendar.description: The base of operations lies beneath Tresendar Manor.

rel_tpl_phandalin_iarno_redbrands.description: Glasstaff leads the Redbrands from the shadows.

rel_tpl_phandalin_iarno_secret.description: His double identity should be revealed through clues, not free exposition.

rel_tpl_phandalin_tresendar_secret.description: Exploring Tresendar can reveal correspondence or proof.

rel_tpl_phandalin_nezznar_wave.description: Nezznar understands the mine’s true value.

rel_tpl_phandalin_wave_gundren.description: The final location depends on Gundren’s map.

rel_tpl_phandalin_redbrands_consequence.description: Resolving the urban arc transforms Phandalin’s behavior.

rel_tpl_phandalin_inn_toblen.description: The inn lets the party hear the town’s fear through Toblen and his customers.

rel_tpl_phandalin_toblen_redbrands.description: Toblen stays quiet because the Redbrands can punish his family.

rel_tpl_phandalin_halia_redbrands.description: Halia wants the Redbrands gone, but not out of altruism.

rel_tpl_phandalin_qelline_hideout.description: Qelline knows people who can point toward less-watched routes.

rel_tpl_phandalin_clay_tresendar.description: The red clay leads toward the path beneath Tresendar.

rel_tpl_phandalin_blackseal_nezznar.description: The black seal suggests someone outside town is directing the conflict.

rel_tpl_phandalin_map_wave.description: The torn map makes the mine a visible promise without revealing the whole route.

rel_tpl_phandalin_break_fear_consequence.description: Breaking the fear lets Phandalin start acting as a community.

rel_tpl_phandalin_street_breakfear.description: The main-street fight can turn a complaint into a concrete quest.

rel_tpl_phandalin_nezznar_secret.description: Nezznar’s influence appears first as money, notes and indirect orders.

rel_tpl_phandalin_shrine_rumor.description: The shrine gathers rumors neighbors will not say in the inn.

rel_tpl_phandalin_linene_trail.description: Linene needs safe roads to keep frontier trade alive.

fact_tpl_phandalin_1.statement: Gundren disappeared before the supplies reached Phandalin.

fact_tpl_phandalin_2.statement: The Redbrands control the town’s fear more than its physical territory.

fact_tpl_phandalin_3.statement: Iarno and Glasstaff are the same person.

fact_tpl_phandalin_4.statement: Wave Echo Cave is not only a mine: it contains a coveted source of magical power.

fact_tpl_phandalin_5.statement: If the party saves Phandalin from the Redbrands, neighbors will start offering information without interrogation.

fact_tpl_phandalin_6.statement: Toblen knows which neighbors pay the Redbrands, but needs to see that the party can protect specific people.

fact_tpl_phandalin_7.statement: Halia can be a temporary ally against the Redbrands, though she will try to fill the power vacuum they leave.

fact_tpl_phandalin_8.statement: The black-seal note proves Glasstaff is under pressure from someone interested in the mine.

fact_tpl_phandalin_9.statement: Gundren’s map works best if handed out incomplete: the table gets a clear promise without skipping the town arc.

fact_tpl_phandalin_10.statement: If the characters resolve a public threat without humiliating the town, neighbors will begin offering voluntary help.

fact_tpl_phandalin_11.statement: Qelline has no political power, but she does have a trusted network that avoids unnecessary fights.

fact_tpl_phandalin_12.statement: Nezznar prefers adventurers wasting time on local trouble before they look toward Wave Echo Cave.

prep.summary: Direct opening: attack, trail, rescue and first emotional link to Gundren’s disappearance.
prep.openingPrompt: The road narrows between trees. Two horses block the path. They do not neigh. They do not move. And the arrows are already ready.
goals: Reveal that Gundren is in danger.
Offer a clear decision: follow the trail or protect the cargo.

chk_ph_1.label: Show the horses before the attack.

chk_ph_2.label: Give the group a visible clue toward the hideout.

prep.summary: Present Phandalin as a living place and prepare the Redbrand arc.
prep.openingPrompt: Phandalin looks quiet until someone lowers their voice when a red cloak appears across the street.
goals: Show the Redbrands as a nearby problem.

chk_ph_3.label: Make the town’s fear visible, not explained.

prep.summary: Resolve the Redbrand arc through infiltration, combat, rescue or negotiation.
prep.openingPrompt: The manor does not look like a fortress. That is the problem: nobody wants to look underneath.
goals: Reveal Glasstaff clues if players investigate.

chk_ph_4.label: Close by asking what changes in Phandalin after the victory.

sess_tpl_phandalin_allies.title: Session 4 — Allies in an awakening town

prep.summary: After striking the Redbrands, the group can turn scattered favors into real town support.
prep.openingPrompt: The next morning, no one looks at the adventurers the same way. Some lower their voices; others, for the first time, approach.
goals: Show consequences of protecting or ignoring specific neighbors.
Deliver a clue toward the larger network behind Glasstaff.

ph_s4_pnjs.label: Choose three NPCs who react to what happened.

ph_s4_public.label: Decide what information is now public for the player portal.

ph_s4_next.label: Close with a clue toward Tresendar or the mine.

sess_tpl_phandalin_wave.title: Session 5 — Echoes of the lost mine

prep.summary: The campaign points toward Wave Echo Cave: promise of treasure, rescue and truth about the Black Spider.
prep.openingPrompt: The map fragment finally matches Gundren’s marks. The route leads not only to a mine, but to an old promise everyone wants to claim.
goals: Connect the local arc to the larger threat.
Prepare the next stretch if the table wants to continue.

ph_s5_map.label: Decide which part of the map is still missing.

ph_s5_nezznar.label: Show one indirect action by Nezznar before revealing him.

ph_s5_continue.label: Prepare a choice: rescue, negotiate or pursue.

cvs_tpl_phandalin_fronts.title: Phandalin fronts
cvs_tpl_phandalin_fronts.description: Quick view of the main threads so you can run without getting lost.

note_tpl_ph_family.title: Family table
note_tpl_ph_family.text: Make decisions visible: help the town, search for Gundren or investigate the manor. Do not hide the fun behind too many names.
